quiz.title: Unit 9 Quiz 4, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Mass of Products from Limiting Reactants
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Mass of Products Calculations, Limiting Reactants, Stoichiometry, Mole Ratios

questions.question: For 2KOH + H₂SO₄ → K₂SO₄ + 2H₂O, 11.2 g KOH and 9.80 g H₂SO₄ react. What mass of K₂SO₄ forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass KOH = 56.1 g/mol, H₂SO₄ = 98.1 g/mol, K₂SO₄ = 174.3 g/mol
Moles KOH = 11.2/56.1 = 0.200 mol (needs 0.100 mol H₂SO₄)
Moles H₂SO₄ = 9.80/98.1 = 0.100 mol
Exact stoichiometry
K₂SO₄ produced = 0.100 mol
Mass = 0.100 × 174.3 = 17.4 g

questions.question: In 4Fe + 3O₂ → 2Fe₂O₃, 28.0 g Fe and 12.0 g O₂ react. What mass of Fe₂O₃ forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass Fe = 55.85 g/mol, O₂ = 32 g/mol, Fe₂O₃ = 159.7 g/mol
Moles Fe = 28.0/55.85 = 0.501 mol (needs 0.376 mol O₂)
Moles O₂ = 12.0/32 = 0.375 mol
O₂ is limiting
Fe₂O₃ from O₂ = 0.375 × (2/3) = 0.250 mol
Mass = 0.250 × 159.7 = 39.9 g ≈ 40.0 g

questions.question: For NH₃ + HCl → NH₄Cl, 8.50 g NH₃ and 18.3 g HCl react. What mass of NH₄Cl forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass NH₃ = 17.03 g/mol, HCl = 36.46 g/mol, NH₄Cl = 53.49 g/mol
Moles NH₃ = 8.50/17.03 = 0.499 mol
Moles HCl = 18.3/36.46 = 0.502 mol
NH₃ is limiting
NH₄Cl produced = 0.499 mol
Mass = 0.499 × 53.49 = 26.7 g ≈ 26.8 g

questions.question: Given 2Al + Fe₂O₃ → 2Fe + Al₂O₃, 5.4 g Al and 16.0 g Fe₂O₃ react. What mass of Fe forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass Al = 27 g/mol, Fe₂O₃ = 160 g/mol, Fe = 55.85 g/mol
Moles Al = 5.4/27 = 0.20 mol (needs 0.10 mol Fe₂O₃)
Moles Fe₂O₃ = 16.0/160 = 0.10 mol
Exact stoichiometry
Fe produced = 0.20 mol
Mass = 0.20 × 55.85 = 11.2 g

questions.question: For 2NaOH + CO₂ → Na₂CO₃ + H₂O, 8.00 g NaOH and 4.40 g CO₂ react. What mass of Na₂CO₃ forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass NaOH = 40.00 g/mol, CO₂ = 44.01 g/mol, Na₂CO₃ = 106.0 g/mol
Moles NaOH = 8.00/40.00 = 0.200 mol (needs 0.100 mol CO₂)
Moles CO₂ = 4.40/44.01 = 0.100 mol
Exact stoichiometry
Na₂CO₃ produced = 0.100 mol
Mass = 0.100 × 106.0 = 10.6 g

questions.question: In Pb(NO₃)₂ + 2NaCl → PbCl₂ + 2NaNO₃, 33.1 g Pb(NO₃)₂ and 11.7 g NaCl react. What mass of PbCl₂ forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass Pb(NO₃)₂ = 331.2 g/mol, NaCl = 58.44 g/mol, PbCl₂ = 278.1 g/mol
Moles Pb(NO₃)₂ = 33.1/331.2 = 0.100 mol (needs 0.200 mol NaCl)
Moles NaCl = 11.7/58.44 = 0.200 mol
Exact stoichiometry
PbCl₂ produced = 0.100 mol
Mass = 0.100 × 278.1 = 27.8 g

questions.question: For C + O₂ → CO₂, 12.0 g C and 24.0 g O₂ react. What mass of CO₂ is produced?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass C = 12.01 g/mol, O₂ = 32 g/mol, CO₂ = 44.01 g/mol
Moles C = 12.0/12.01 = 0.999 mol
Moles O₂ = 24.0/32 = 0.75 mol
O₂ is limiting (needs 0.999 mol O₂ for 1:1 ratio)
CO₂ produced = 0.75 mol
Mass = 0.75 × 44.01 = 33.0 g

questions.question: Given 2H₂S + SO₂ → 3S + 2H₂O, 6.8 g H₂S and 6.4 g SO₂ react. What mass of sulfur forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass H₂S = 34.08 g/mol, SO₂ = 64.07 g/mol, S = 32.07 g/mol
Moles H₂S = 6.8/34.08 = 0.20 mol (needs 0.10 mol SO₂)
Moles SO₂ = 6.4/64.07 = 0.10 mol
Exact stoichiometry
S produced = 0.20 × (3/2) = 0.30 mol
Mass = 0.30 × 32.07 = 9.62 g ≈ 9.6 g

questions.question: For BaCl₂ + Na₂SO₄ → BaSO₄ + 2NaCl, 20.8 g BaCl₂ and 14.2 g Na₂SO₄ react. What mass of BaSO₄ forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass BaCl₂ = 208.2 g/mol, Na₂SO₄ = 142.0 g/mol, BaSO₄ = 233.4 g/mol
Moles BaCl₂ = 20.8/208.2 = 0.100 mol
Moles Na₂SO₄ = 14.2/142.0 = 0.100 mol
Exact stoichiometry
BaSO₄ produced = 0.100 mol
Mass = 0.100 × 233.4 = 23.3 g

questions.question: In 2AgNO₃ + Cu → 2Ag + Cu(NO₃)₂, 34.0 g AgNO₃ and 3.2 g Cu react. What mass of Ag forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass AgNO₃ = 170 g/mol, Cu = 63.55 g/mol, Ag = 107.9 g/mol
Moles AgNO₃ = 34.0/170 = 0.20 mol (needs 0.10 mol Cu)
Moles Cu = 3.2/63.55 = 0.050 mol
Cu is limiting
Ag produced = 0.050 × 2 = 0.10 mol
Mass = 0.10 × 107.9 = 10.8 g

questions.question: For 3Cu + 8HNO₃ → 3Cu(NO₃)₂ + 2NO + 4H₂O, 19.2 g Cu and 63.0 g HNO₃ react. What mass of Cu(NO₃)₂ forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass Cu = 63.55 g/mol, HNO₃ = 63.01 g/mol, Cu(NO₃)₂ = 187.6 g/mol
Moles Cu = 19.2/63.55 = 0.302 mol (needs 0.806 mol HNO₃)
Moles HNO₃ = 63.0/63.01 = 1.00 mol
Cu is limiting
Cu(NO₃)₂ produced = 0.302 mol
Mass = 0.302 × 187.6 = 56.7 g

questions.question: Given TiCl₄ + 2Mg → Ti + 2MgCl₂, 38.0 g TiCl₄ and 24.0 g Mg react. What mass of Ti forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass TiCl₄ = 189.7 g/mol, Mg = 24.3 g/mol, Ti = 47.87 g/mol
Moles TiCl₄ = 38.0/189.7 = 0.200 mol (needs 0.400 mol Mg)
Moles Mg = 24.0/24.3 = 0.988 mol
TiCl₄ is limiting
Ti produced = 0.200 mol
Mass = 0.200 × 47.87 = 9.57 g ≈ 9.60 g

questions.question: For 2KClO₃ → 2KCl + 3O₂, 24.5 g KClO₃ decomposes with 10.0 g catalyst MnO₂. What mass of O₂ forms?
questions.explanation:
Catalyst MnO₂ is not consumed
Molar mass KClO₃ = 122.55 g/mol, O₂ = 32 g/mol
Moles KClO₃ = 24.5/122.55 = 0.200 mol
O₂ produced = 0.200 × (3/2) = 0.300 mol
Mass = 0.300 × 32 = 9.60 g

questions.question: For Fe₂O₃ + 3CO → 2Fe + 3CO₂, 80.0 g Fe₂O₃ and 42.0 g CO react. What mass of Fe forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass Fe₂O₃ = 160 g/mol, CO = 28 g/mol, Fe = 55.85 g/mol
Moles Fe₂O₃ = 80.0/160 = 0.500 mol (needs 1.50 mol CO)
Moles CO = 42.0/28 = 1.50 mol
Exact stoichiometry
Fe produced = 0.500 × 2 = 1.00 mol
Mass = 1.00 × 55.85 = 55.9 g ≈ 56.0 g

questions.question: Given 2C₂H₂ + 5O₂ → 4CO₂ + 2H₂O, 13.0 g C₂H₂ and 40.0 g O₂ react. What mass of CO₂ forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass C₂H₂ = 26.04 g/mol, O₂ = 32 g/mol, CO₂ = 44.01 g/mol
Moles C₂H₂ = 13.0/26.04 = 0.499 mol (needs 1.25 mol O₂)
Moles O₂ = 40.0/32 = 1.25 mol
Exact stoichiometry
CO₂ produced = 0.499 × 2 = 0.998 mol
Mass = 0.998 × 44.01 = 43.9 g ≈ 44.0 g

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered product mass calculations from limiting reactants! 🧪⚖️
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review identifying limiting reactants and using mole ratios.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on converting mass to moles correctly.
scoreMessages.0: Please review stoichiometry, limiting reactants, and mass-to-mole conversions.
